title: Create concurrency-specific onboarding tour вҖ” 4 steps from sidebar to first animation
description:
CRITICAL GAP: The platform's OnboardingOverlay (onboarding-overlay.tsx) has 5 steps but ALL target System Design module (component palette, canvas, simulation). When a user navigates to Concurrency, there is ZERO onboarding. They see 11 demo buttons, playback controls, and a static visualization with no guidance.

Create a concurrency-specific tour using the same OnboardingOverlay pattern:

Step 1 (center, no target): 'Welcome to the Concurrency Lab. Here you'll visualize race conditions, deadlocks, and thread synchronization вҖ” step by step.'

Step 2 (target: concurrency sidebar demo list): 'Pick a concept from this list. Start with Race Condition вҖ” it's the most fundamental concurrency bug.'

Step 3 (target: playback controls area): 'Use Play to animate, Step to advance one tick, or press Space for a keyboard shortcut. Watch the visualization update in real time.'

Step 4 (target: properties panel): 'Each concept includes an explanation, real-world connections, and live state tracking. Read this panel to understand WHY things happen, not just WHAT.'

Trigger: when user switches to Concurrency module for the first time (check localStorage 'concurrency_tour_completed'). Skip button always visible. Replay from Settings.
acceptanceCriteria: 4-step concurrency tour with spotlight targets on sidebar, controls, and properties panel
Tour triggers on first visit to Concurrency module (not on every visit)
Tour uses existing OnboardingOverlay component pattern with data-onboarding attributes
Skip button on every step, replay from Settings
After tour completes, the first demo (Race Condition) is selected and ready to Play

title: Add 'Show Me' auto-play overlay on canvas initial state for every demo
description:
FIRST SUCCESS BARRIER: When a user selects any concurrency demo, the canvas shows the initial state (counter=0, empty buffer, philosophers thinking) with no indication of what to do. The Play button is in the sidebar footer вҖ” far from the canvas.

Add a prominent overlay on the canvas initial state (stepIndex===0 && !playing):

```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ                                                  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ   в–¶  Show Me How Race Conditions Work            в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                                                  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ   Click to watch 3 threads compete for a         в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ   shared counter вҖ” and see what goes wrong.      в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                                                  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ         [ в–¶ Show Me ]    [ I'll Step Through ]   в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                                                  в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

- 'Show Me' auto-plays at 1x speed
- 'I'll Step Through' dismisses the overlay and lets user use manual controls
- Overlay disappears after first interaction (Play/Step/dismiss)
- Different text per demo (Race Condition, Producer-Consumer, etc.)
- Does not reappear for the same demo after first viewing (localStorage per-demo flag)
acceptanceCriteria: Overlay visible on canvas when stepIndex===0 and not playing
Demo-specific text for all 12 demos
'Show Me' button triggers auto-play at 1x speed
Overlay disappears after first interaction
Per-demo localStorage flag prevents re-showing

title: Add sidebar 'Start Here' badge and recommended order for new users
description:
The sidebar shows 11 demos in a flat list with no indication of where to start. A new user doesn't know if they should start with Race Condition or Lock Comparison.

ADD:
1. 'Start Here вҶ’' badge on Race Condition (first demo in recommended order)
2. Subtle numbered badges (1, 2, 3...) showing recommended order: Race Condition вҶ’ Producer-Consumer вҶ’ Dining Philosophers вҶ’ Event Loop вҶ’ Thread Lifecycle
3. 'Beginner' / 'Intermediate' / 'Advanced' section headers grouping the demos
4. Check mark on demos that have been viewed (from MODULE_FEATURES progress tracking)

Badge disappears after the user has viewed 3+ demos (no longer a 'new' user).
acceptanceCriteria: 'Start Here вҶ’' badge visible on Race Condition for new users
Demos grouped into Beginner/Intermediate/Advanced sections
Check marks appear on viewed demos (from progress tracking)
Badges disappear after 3+ demos viewed

title: Add contextual tooltips to concurrency playback controls (Play, Step, Reset, speed)
description:
The contextual tooltip system (ContextualTooltip.tsx with useFirstEncounter hook) exists but has ZERO concurrency entries. The 4 platform tooltips all target System Design UI elements.

Add concurrency-specific tooltips that appear on FIRST encounter:
1. Play button: 'Animate the simulation step by step. Press Space as a shortcut.'
2. Step button: 'Advance one tick at a time. Perfect for understanding each operation.'
3. Reset button: 'Return to the beginning. Try a different demo or watch again.'
4. Demo selector: 'Each demo visualizes a different concurrency concept. Start with Race Condition.'
5. Properties panel: 'Read here for explanations, real-world connections, and live state tracking.'

Use the existing CONTEXTUAL_FEATURES pattern вҖ” add entries and place data-onboarding attributes on the controls.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 5 concurrency contextual tooltips defined in CONTEXTUAL_FEATURES or a concurrency-specific array
Tooltips appear on first encounter only (useFirstEncounter hook)
data-onboarding attributes added to Play, Step, Reset buttons and demo selector
Tooltips position correctly relative to their targets

title: Add idle detection вҖ” show help prompt after 15s of no interaction on first visit
description:
STUCK DETECTION: If a new user sits on the Concurrency module for 15+ seconds without clicking anything (sidebar, play, step), they're likely confused. Show a gentle prompt:

'Not sure where to start? Click the в–¶ Play button to watch a race condition happen in real time. Or press Space.'

Implement as a setTimeout that resets on any click/keypress. Only triggers once per session. Only on first module visit (check localStorage).
acceptanceCriteria: Prompt appears after 15 seconds of no interaction on first visit
Prompt dismisses on any click, keypress, or explicit dismiss
Prompt does not reappear after dismissal (session-scoped)
Only triggers on first-ever Concurrency module visit (localStorage flag)

title: Add properties panel empty state with concept preview and CTA
description:
The properties panel shows concept explanations AFTER a demo is selected. But the initial load also shows Race Condition content immediately (because it's the default). For demos OTHER than the default, the properties panel content is static text вҖ” no interactive CTA.

Rewrite the properties panel for each demo to include:
1. HOOK sentence (from CON-125-129 concept rewrites) as the first thing visible
2. 'Learn More' expandable section for detailed explanation
3. A CTA at the bottom: 'Try it: Click в–¶ Play or press Space to see this concept in action'
4. After the demo has been played, the CTA changes to: 'Try Next: [next recommended demo name] вҶ’'
acceptanceCriteria: Properties panel opens with hook sentence for every demo
Detailed explanation is in a collapsible 'Learn More' section
CTA button at bottom of properties panel links to Play action
After completion, CTA suggests next demo in learning order

title: Add 'Replay Tour' and 'Keyboard Shortcuts' entries to concurrency module help menu
description:
The platform has a keyboard-shortcuts-dialog.tsx and settings page. But there's no way to replay the concurrency onboarding tour after it's been dismissed.

Add:
1. 'Replay Tour' button in the sidebar header or settings that resets the localStorage flag and triggers the tour
2. Concurrency-specific keyboard shortcuts registered in the keyboard shortcuts dialog (Play=Space, Step=вҶ’, Step Back=вҶҗ, Reset=R)
3. A '?' help icon in the sidebar header that shows:
   - 'Replay Tour'
   - 'Keyboard Shortcuts' (opens dialog)
   - 'About Concurrency Lab' (brief module description)
acceptanceCriteria: '?' help icon visible in concurrency sidebar header
Clicking '?' shows dropdown with Replay Tour, Keyboard Shortcuts, About
Replay Tour resets localStorage flag and triggers onboarding tour
Concurrency shortcuts appear in the global keyboard shortcuts dialog

title: Add mobile-specific onboarding for concurrency вҖ” swipe hint and bottom nav guidance
description:
The activity bar renders as a horizontal bottom nav on mobile (MOBILE_VISIBLE_COUNT = 5 at activity-bar.tsx:61). But the concurrency module (shortcut='9') is NOT in the first 5 вҖ” it's behind the 'more' overflow menu. A mobile user must: tap '...', find Concurrency, tap it. Then they see the module with no mobile-specific guidance.

Add:
1. Mobile discovery: if user hasn't tried Concurrency, show a periodic 'Explore more modules вҶ’' badge on the overflow '...' button
2. Once in Concurrency on mobile: show a swipe hint ('Swipe left/right to switch demos')
3. Properties panel accessible via swipe-up gesture (it's a BottomSheet on mobile per workspace-layout.tsx:14)
4. Playback controls should be visible without scrolling on mobile (currently in sidebar footer which is hidden on mobile)
acceptanceCriteria: Overflow '...' button shows badge when unvisited modules exist
Swipe hint appears on first mobile visit to Concurrency
Playback controls are accessible on mobile without opening sidebar
Properties BottomSheet has a peek indicator showing it contains content
